blocks.task: Analyze db.js join execution path. Map exactly where nested loop happens and design hash join integration.
blocks.summary: Mapped join path: db.js _executeJoin lines 1623-1637 does nested loop with full heap scan per left row. Need to detect equi-join conditions from join.on AST, build hash map on right table join column, probe for each left row. O(n*m) -> O(n+m). Filter condition: join.on is a BinaryExpression with = operator and column refs from different tables.
blocks.details: Mapped join path: db.js _executeJoin lines 1623-1637 does nested loop with full heap scan per left row. Need to detect equi-join conditions from join.on AST, build hash map on right table join column, probe for each left row. O(n*m) -> O(n+m). Filter condition: join.on is a BinaryExpression with = operator and column refs from different tables.

blocks.task: Reflect on evening explorations. What was surprising?
blocks.summary: Key surprises: 1) PG snapshot is xmin:xmax:xip_list not just a timestamp — fixed a real correctness bug in our MVCC. 2) Hint bits: reads can write (lazy caching of commit status on tuple header). 3) Vectorized doesnt help in JS (V8 JIT handles polymorphism well, 3x slower for simple ops). Lesson: read real implementations before building your own.
